definition: Users cannot find billing-related settings, plans, or payment controls after signup or during early setup.
include: Billing settings are hard to locate; Payment or subscription controls are buried in menus; Users say they had to hunt for billing
exclude: General confusion about pricing before signup; Problems paying due to a technical error; Lack of billing access because of permissions

definition: Users cannot easily find places to manage core account settings such as teammates, permissions, or similar administrative controls.
include: Hunting through menus to manage account-related settings; Difficulty finding team, permission, or admin controls; Need for a single place to manage key account tasks
exclude: Issues with the invite flow itself once opened; Problems unrelated to account management; General navigation unfamiliarity without a specific settings target

definition: Users struggle to understand how to invite teammates or add collaborators during onboarding.
include: Invitation process is unclear; Users cannot figure out how to add teammates; Collaboration setup creates early friction
exclude: Permission management after teammates are already added; General account settings discoverability without invite context

definition: Support responds quickly, but the response does not resolve the user’s onboarding blocker or account issue.
include: Quick support reply that fails to fix the issue; User still blocked after contacting support; Support answers are not actionable
exclude: Slow support response; No support available; Self-serve help articles that are hard to find

definition: Users remain unable to access their account or continue onboarding because the underlying access problem is not fixed.
include: Login or access issue remains unresolved; User cannot proceed due to account access problems; Onboarding stalls because access is blocked
exclude: General confusion about where to click; Feature discoverability issues not tied to access

definition: Users need too much time to learn how to move around the product or understand where things are located.
include: Navigation takes time to learn; Users are not immediately oriented; Menu structure is hard to remember
exclude: Specific settings are hidden but navigation is otherwise learned; Search is unavailable or broken; Invite or billing tasks that are clearly identified but hard to complete

definition: Users have to search through menus to find functions or settings instead of reaching them through a clear path.
include: Hunting through menus; Multiple clicks to locate tasks; Lack of obvious entry points
exclude: Unable to find a specific billing or account setting when the issue is really discoverability of that feature; General desire for consolidation without explicit menu searching

definition: Onboarding instructions or checklists are easy to understand and help users complete initial setup.
include: Checklist is clear; Instructions are easy to follow; Onboarding guidance reduces uncertainty
exclude: Checklist is present but users cannot find related settings; Emails that duplicate completed steps

definition: The product provides immediate visibility into progress or status, helping users feel oriented early in onboarding.
include: Dashboard shows progress quickly; Users can immediately see what has happened; Early feedback or status visibility
exclude: General performance speed without progress feedback; Reports being useful but not about progress

definition: The product responds quickly or feels speedy during use.
include: Product feels fast; Responsive interface; Low-latency interaction

definition: Reports provide valuable information to the user during onboarding or early use.
include: Reports are useful; Analytics or reports help users understand value
exclude: Progress visibility on the dashboard; General dashboard satisfaction

definition: Onboarding emails are perceived as useful guidance for new users.
include: Emails help users understand steps; Email guidance supports onboarding
exclude: In-app guidance; Duplicate or redundant messaging

definition: Onboarding emails or other guidance repeat actions the user has already completed, creating redundancy and potential frustration.
include: Emails repeat completed in-app steps; Duplicate instructions across channels; Redundant onboarding prompts
exclude: Helpful reminders for incomplete tasks; Clear checklist guidance without repetition
